introSection: Thunderbolt is a wooden roller coaster located at Six Flags New England in Agawam, Massachusetts, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Cyclone opened at the 1939 New York World's Fair on May 5, 1939. It operated there through the fair's ending in 1940.

The ride was relocated to Riverside Park (now known as Six Flags New England) for the 1941 season. Joseph E. Drambour was involved in the ride's reconstruction. The ride was renamed Thunderbolt its second season at Riverside Park.

The ride is currently the oldest roller coaster at a Six Flags park. On August 2, 2008, the ride was dedicated an ACE Roller Coaster Landmark by the American Coaster Enthusiasts.

fullText:
Thunderbolt is a wooden roller coaster located at Six Flags New England in Agawam, Massachusetts, USA.

Cyclone opened at the 1939 New York World's Fair on May 5, 1939. It operated there through the fair's ending in 1940.

The ride was relocated to Riverside Park (now known as Six Flags New England) for the 1941 season. Joseph E. Drambour was involved in the ride's reconstruction. The ride was renamed Thunderbolt its second season at Riverside Park.

The ride is currently the oldest roller coaster at a Six Flags park. On August 2, 2008, the ride was dedicated an ACE Roller Coaster Landmark by the American Coaster Enthusiasts.



White track and supports.






 * Thunderbolt on the parks website
